payload.executiveSummary: The teacher clearly modeled proportional reasoning with unit-rate examples, but the strongest next move is tightening the check for understanding before independent practice.
whatWentWell: The worked example connected the ratio table to a real classroom scenario.
Students were prompted to explain why the unit rate stayed constant across examples.
The teacher revisited academic vocabulary before moving into guided practice.
whatCanImprove: The transition into independent work came before every student demonstrated the conversion process.
The teacher could require more full-sentence student explanations after each ratio comparison.
Exit-check evidence stayed mostly verbal instead of showing who could solve independently.
contentGapsToReinforce: Students need one more explicit bridge from equivalent ratios to the unit-rate shortcut.
The lesson would benefit from a clearer misconception check around dividing both quantities by the same factor.
payload.recommendedNextStep: Before the next lesson segment, add a two-problem mastery check that requires every student to compute and justify a unit rate independently.

instructionalCoachingFeedback.label: Key Findings
instructionalCoachingFeedback.content: Modeling was strong, but independent evidence of mastery stayed thin.
bullets: The teacher used a consistent ratio table structure.
Students had limited written accountability before release.

instructionalCoachingFeedback.label: Missed Opportunities
instructionalCoachingFeedback.content: The strongest missed move was a sharper misconception check before independent practice.

instructionalCoachingFeedback.label: Student Engagement Signals
instructionalCoachingFeedback.content: Students were verbally responsive during guided examples.
bullets: Several students answered when asked to compare ratios aloud.

instructionalCoachingFeedback.label: Suggested Next Steps
instructionalCoachingFeedback.content: Use a visible mini-check before release.
bullets: Have every student solve one ratio conversion and justify it in writing.

teksStandardsAlignment.label: Covered in the Lesson
teksStandardsAlignment.content: The core ratio/unit-rate expectation was clearly addressed.
bullets: 6.4H: convert units within a measurement system
6.4E: represent ratios and rates with tables and verbal descriptions

teksStandardsAlignment.label: Needs Reinforcement
teksStandardsAlignment.content: Independent assessment evidence needs to be stronger.
bullets: 6.4E: students still need stronger evidence of independent reasoning

staarTeksCoverage.label: Readiness Summary
staarTeksCoverage.content: Readiness coverage was solid but not fully verified independently.
